claim: On June 24, 2021, at approximately 1:22 a.m. EDT, Champlain Towers South — a 12-story beachfront condominium at 8777 Collins Avenue in Surfside, Florida — partially collapsed, killing 98 people and ranking among the deadliest structural disasters in U.S. history. NIST's preliminary investigation has identified the pool deck as the likely origin of the collapse, with long-term water penetration and corrosion of reinforced concrete in the underground parking structure as contributing factors; problems had been reported in 2018 and described as “much worse” in April 2021. The collapse occurred approximately 24 hours after software pioneer John McAfee was found dead in a Spanish prison cell on June 23, 2021. A widely shared screenshot circulating in the days after the collapse purported to show a June 8, 2021 McAfee tweet stating that 31 terabytes of files were stored “in my condo near 88th Street and Collins Avenue just north of Miami Beach” — the address of Champlain Towers South. The Internet Archive's Wayback Machine captured McAfee's Twitter timeline on June 20, 2021, twelve days after the alleged tweet, and the supposed tweet does not appear; no contemporaneous quote-tweet, reply, or news mention from the June 8-22 window references it; and Miami-Dade property records show McAfee did not own a unit at Champlain Towers. PolitiFact, Snopes, BOOM Live, and Misbar each independently checked and concluded the screenshot was not authentic. McAfee did, however, make a separate, real June 2019 “dead man's switch” tweet from Cuba threatening to release 31+ terabytes of incriminating data on government corruption if he were arrested or disappeared — that earlier tweet is documented under the McAfee entry on this page.

sources.label: Wikipedia — “Surfside condominium collapse”
sources.quote: On June 24, 2021, at approximately 1:22 a.m. EDT, Champlain Towers South, a 12-story beachfront condominium in the Miami suburb of Surfside, Florida, United States, partially collapsed, causing the deaths of 98 people.

Fight Back Podcast / @jakeshieldspod, Instagram reel DRoJ9FFiSUv (Nov 28, 2025) — surfacing source

notes: NIST's ongoing technical investigation has identified pool-deck origin and reinforced-concrete degradation as the leading cause hypothesis; this is the documented structural-engineering record. The McAfee-Champlain connection narrative is now threaded through both this record and 2021-mcafee-death-001 so a reader landing on either entry sees the full chain: real timing (24-hour gap), real 2019 dead-man's-switch tweet, and a June 2021 Champlain-Towers tweet that does not appear in any archive snapshot postdating its alleged June 8 posting date, with no contemporaneous quote-tweet or news mention, and no property-records connection. The Wayback Machine snapshot of June 20, 2021 is the load-bearing primary check; the honest evidentiary caveat is that the Wayback density during the relevant window has an 18-day gap between June 2 and June 20, so the alleged tweet could only have existed if posted and deleted entirely within that gap with no archive or downstream propagation surviving. The 8777 Collins Avenue address is correct for Champlain Towers South; the fabricated tweet's '88th Street and Collins Avenue' phrasing is geographically adjacent (the tower sits near the corner of 88th Street and Collins Avenue), which is part of what made the screenshot superficially plausible.
